steps.title: Understand what the reference to 'anvil and strife' does in context
steps.content:
The friend tells the speaker his soul is calm "by the lack of anvil and strife." "Anvil and strife" represent noise, work, and conflict. The friend defines his tranquility negatively — by naming what is absent. This creates a contrast: his present peaceful state is thrown into sharp relief by invoking its opposite.
  • Choice A — ‘It emphasizes the way that nature provides a contrast to the feelings of the speaker and her friend.’: The contrast is between the friend's tranquility and the concept of conflict, not between nature and human feelings. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It illustrates how the speaker and her friend understand each other's feelings without having to talk about them.’: The friend does speak aloud here; this choice misreads the passage's "deeper content was never spoken" as applying to this moment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It represents a strong contrast to the speaker's friend's current experience of tranquility.’: Correct — anvil and strife (noise, conflict) are the opposite of the friend's calm, and naming them by their absence highlights that tranquility through contrast. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It symbolizes the speaker's friend's view that meaningful work and social engagement are core components of a fulfilling life.’: The friend describes their absence as the source of his calm, not as values he holds dear. This reverses the poem's meaning. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
The reference to "anvil and strife" works by contrast — invoking the opposite of tranquility to define and emphasize the friend's current peace. The correct response captures this rhetorical function precisely.

That confirms C is correct.
